explanations.15: The spread operator [...updates] creates a shallow copy so we don't modify the original array when we sort it.
explanations.16: This converts date strings into Date objects so we can subtract them and sort from newest (b) to oldest (a).
explanations.22: The aria-hidden="true" tells screen readers to ignore the emoji because it's just decorative, not essential information.
explanations.27: We combine the date and index (i) as the key because using just the date might cause issues if multiple updates have the same date.
explanations.33: The dateTime attribute stores the actual machine-readable date while the displayed text shows a user-friendly format.
explanations.34: toLocaleDateString converts the date string into the user's language and format based on their locale setting.
explanations.42: dangerouslySetInnerHTML is a special React prop that lets you insert raw HTML, here because parseMarkdown returns HTML instead of plain text.
